en_title: 'It Is Not Realistic for Us to Absorb All Component Cost Increases' — Sony Says It Won't Sell Hardware at a 'Significant' Loss as Concern Grows Over PS6 Price
cn_title: 索尼称不会以重大亏损卖硬件，PS6定价引担忧
subtitle: 不差钱
opus_summary: 索尼CEO西野秀明表示不会亏本卖硬件，PS6定价或超成本。受芯片危机影响，PS5已最高涨至899美元(约合人民币6113元)，XBOX也将再涨。

paragraphs.en: Sony has insisted it has no plans to sell hardware at a “significant” loss, with concern growing over the potential price of the PlayStation 6 amid the ongoing RAMpocalypse.
paragraphs.cn: 索尼坚称不打算以「重大」亏损销售硬件，随着当前「内存危机」持续，外界对PlayStation 6潜在定价的担忧日益加剧。

paragraphs.en: Current generation consoles have gone up in price, with Sony, Microsoft, and Nintendo struggling to cope with the component shortage fueled by the AI boom. Sony raised the price of PS5 consoles in April , bringing the cost of a standard PS5 to $649.99, the PS5 Digital Edition to $599.99, and the PS5 Pro to $899.99. It blamed the move on “continued pressures in the global economic landscape.”
paragraphs.cn: 当前世代的主机价格已经上涨，索尼、微软和任天堂都在努力应对由AI热潮引发的元器件短缺。索尼在4月上调了PS5的售价，标准版PS5涨至649.99美元(约合人民币4420元)，PS5数字版涨至599.99美元(约合人民币4080元)，PS5 Pro涨至899.99美元(约合人民币6120元)。索尼将此归咎于「全球经济形势的持续压力」。

paragraphs.en: Just last week, Microsoft announced significant Xbox price rises of its own , which kick in this August. They come less than a year after the last Xbox price rises in October 2025. “We hoped another price increase would not be necessary, and we have spent the last several months working with suppliers on options,” Microsoft explained. “Unfortunately, console storage and memory prices have increased by more than 2.5x and we expect another doubling by the fall of 2027. The entire consumer electronics industry is struggling with the current components crisis, but the effects are particularly hard on consoles. Unlike phones, computers, speakers, and other consumer devices, consoles are typically not sold at a profit, but instead for less than they cost to make.”
paragraphs.cn: 就在上周，微软也宣布了自家XBOX的重大涨价，将于今年8月生效。这距离上次2025年10月的XBOX涨价还不到一年。微软解释称：「我们曾希望不需再次涨价，过去几个月我们一直在与供应商探讨方案。不幸的是，主机存储和内存价格已上涨超过2.5倍，我们预计到2027年秋季还会再翻一番。整个消费电子行业都在与当前的元器件危机作斗争，但对主机的影响尤为严重。与手机、电脑、音箱等消费设备不同，主机通常不是以盈利为目的销售，而是以低于制造成本的价格出售。」

paragraphs.en: There is not only concern that Sony will be forced to raise the price of PS5 yet again, but that the next-generation of consoles — the so far unannounced PS6 and Microsoft’s Project Helix — with cost at least $1,000 unless both companies are willing to take a significant loss on each console sold.
paragraphs.cn: 人们不仅担心索尼将被迫再次上调PS5价格，还担心下一代主机——迄今尚未公布的PS6和微软的Project Helix——售价至少为1000美元(约合人民币6800元)，除非两家公司愿意在每台售价上承受重大亏损。

paragraphs.en: In a recent Q&A with Sony Interactive Entertainment president and CEO, Hideaki Nishino, published in English by Sony itself, the executive suggested taking a big hit on next-gen consoles isn’t something PlayStation will do.
paragraphs.cn: 在索尼自己发布的一份英文问答中，索尼互动娱乐总裁兼CEO西野秀明暗示，让次世代主机承受大幅亏损并非PlayStation会做的事。

paragraphs.en: “Considering consumer behavior, could you update us on your current thinking regarding hardware pricing and profitability? For the next-generation platform, is it reasonable to assume that your pricing will continue to prioritize profitability of the hardware, as it does today?
paragraphs.cn: 「考虑到消费者行为，能否向我们介绍一下你们目前在硬件定价和盈利能力方面的思考？对于次世代平台，我们是否可以合理假设定价将继续以硬件盈利为优先，就像现在这样？」

paragraphs.en: “First, we regard hardware as the base for providing the gaming experience, and by offering products such as the PlayStation Portal Remote Player (PS Portal), we aim to provide experiences tailored to users’ play styles beyond the living room, which has traditionally been considered the primary usage environment.
paragraphs.cn: 「首先，我们将硬件视为提供游戏体验的基础，通过推出 PlayStation Portal Remote Player（PS Portal）等产品，我们旨在提供超越客厅——这一传统上被视为主要使用环境——的、适应玩家游玩风格的体验。

paragraphs.en: “As for pricing, it is not realistic for us to absorb all component cost increases, and we have already implemented some price increases outside Japan. At present, however, sales are proceeding as planned, and we do not believe this has led to a decline in customer demand.
paragraphs.cn: 「在定价方面，我们无法吸收所有零部件成本的上涨，这并不现实，而且我们已经在日本以外地区实施了一些提价。不过，目前销售仍按计划推进，我们不认为这导致了消费者需求的下降。

paragraphs.en: “As a principle, we do not intend to sell hardware at significant losses. At the same time, we are carefully monitoring the market and continuing to evaluate our approach. We believe it is important for us to make every effort to ensure that customers fully understand the value we provide in relation to pricing.”
paragraphs.cn: 「原则上，我们不打算以重大亏损销售硬件。与此同时，我们正密切关注市场，并持续评估策略。我们认为，重要的是尽一切努力让消费者充分理解我们相对于定价所提供的价值。」

paragraphs.en: There are a few things worth unpacking here. Nishino’s mention of the PlayStation Portal and providing "experiences tailored to users’ play styles beyond the living room," could be taken as yet another hint at the long-rumored PS6 handheld or hybrid console . There’s also an acknowledgement that Sony will pass on some component cost increases to the consumer, and has done so already with those PS5 price rises. In short, gamers are going to have to take the hit here.
paragraphs.cn: 这里面有几处值得剖析。西野提到 PlayStation Portal 以及提供「超越客厅的、适应玩家游玩风格的体验」，这或许可被视为对传闻已久的 PS6 掌机或混合主机（hybrid console）的又一暗示。另外，索尼也承认会将部分零部件成本上涨转嫁给消费者，并且已经通过 PS5 涨价落实了这一点。简而言之，玩家得为此买单。

paragraphs.en: Then there’s the suggestion that sales are proceeding as planned despite these price rises. Data from the U.S. perhaps suggests otherwise. As IGN reported last week, May was a catastrophic month for PlayStation and Xbox sales in the U.S. , driven by price rises that have clearly had a significant impact on consumer interest in buying a new console. According to new Circana data, PS5 spending fell 43% year-on-year, and unit sales plummeted 58%. In fact, PlayStation hardware unit sales fell to their lowest May total since May 2000, and Xbox hardware unit sales were the lowest ever recorded for a May month in the U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 而且，尽管有这些涨价，销售仍按计划推进的说法也存在。但美国的数据或许显示出相反情况。正如 IGN 上周报道的那样，5 月对美国 PlayStation 和 XBOX 的销售而言是灾难性的一个月，涨价显然已经对消费者购买新主机的兴趣产生了重大影响。据 Circana 最新数据，PS5 消费支出同比下降 43%，销量暴跌 58%。事实上，PlayStation 硬件销量跌至自 2000 年 5 月以来的最低 5 月总量，而 XBOX 硬件销量则创下美国有史以来 5 月单月的最低纪录。

paragraphs.en: While Sony struggles to deal with the RAMpocalypse, it has said it has yet to decide when to launch the PS6 . The near six-year-old PS5 is approaching the 100 million sold mark (93.7 million), although it continues to lag behind the PS4. It's worth remembering that PS4 had seen various price cuts at this point in its lifecycle, making it a compelling casual purchase. This generation, however, has swung the other way, limiting the potential install base. There are even concerns that Sony and Microsoft won’t be able to release enough consoles into the retail channel to meet the demand this Christmas fueled by the guaranteed system seller that is GTA 6. During a financial Q&A in May, Sony boss Hiroki Totoki insisted that Sony had enough materials to see it through the rest of 2026.
paragraphs.cn: 索尼正在艰难应对内存价格危机（RAMpocalypse）之际，其表示尚未决定何时推出 PS6。即将年满六周岁的 PS5 正接近 1 亿销量大关（9370 万台），但仍落后于 PS4。值得注意的是，PS4 在生命周期相同阶段已多次降价，使其成为极具吸引力的冲动型消费选择。然而，本世代却走向了相反方向，限制了潜在装机量。甚至有人担心，索尼和微软可能无法在圣诞季向零售渠道供应足够的主机，以满足由《GTA6》——这一铁定能带动主机销售的大作——所引爆的需求。在 5 月的一次财务问答中，索尼总裁十时裕树坚称，索尼有足够的物料支撑 2026 年剩余时间的生产。

paragraphs.en: It’s also worth noting recent reports that Sony may have to delay the release of the PS6 to 2028 or even 2029 as a result of the AI-fueled chip crisis. In January, an analyst report suggested that Sony may push the launch of the PS6 beyond 2028 and lengthen the PS5 lifecycle . David Gibson, senior analyst at MST international who focuses on game and tech companies, predicted that “rising memory prices will not impact short-term performance thanks to Sony’s existing inventory.” However, he noted that increased memory costs could become an issue for Sony in the next fiscal year (ending in March 2027), saying “Sony might pass future cost increases onto consumers.”
paragraphs.cn: 同样值得留意的是，近期有报道称，由于AI驱动的芯片危机，索尼可能不得不将PS6的发布推迟到2028年甚至2029年。今年1月，一份分析师报告曾暗示，索尼可能将PS6的发售时间推至2028年以后，并延长PS5的生命周期。专注于游戏与科技公司的MST International资深分析师David Gibson预测：「内存价格上涨不会影响短期业绩，这得益于索尼现有的库存。」但他也指出，内存成本上升可能在下个财年（截至2027年3月）成为索尼的问题，并表示「索尼可能会将未来的成本增长转嫁给消费者」。

paragraphs.en: Wesley is Director, News at IGN. Find him on Twitter at @wyp100. You can reach Wesley at wesley_yinpoole@ign.com or confidentially at wyp100@proton.me.
paragraphs.cn: [MANUAL_TRANSLATION_REQUIRED] Wesley is Director, News at IGN. Find him on Twitter at @wyp100. You can reach Wesley at wesley_yinpoole@ign.com or confidentially at wyp100@proton.me.
